channel.title: The Grant Desk - AI & Safety, Worldwide, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: AI & Safety, Worldwide, added in last 30 days.

item.title: AIxDESIGN: Grain, Open Call for Critical AI Literacy Recipes
item.description:
Organisation: AIxDESIGN, co-produced with the Netherlands Film Academy

Location: Remote/online; open to educators and practitioners based anywhere

Award: Need-based fee of EUR 150 per recipe for freelance and independent practitioners (email hello@aixdesign.co if the time would be hard to make without it). Your recipe is formatted and credited to you in the first edition of Grain, plus a print copy and an invite to the launch. Published under CC BY-SA 4.0; you keep authorship.

Duration: One-off contribution; Grain publishes March 2027

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

Grain is a cookbook of critical and creative AI literacy exercises for educators across audiovisual and creative practice, co-produced by AIxDESIGN and the Netherlands Film Academy and led by Nadia Piet. The open call collects 'recipes': tried-and-tested group exercises for the classroom or workshop that get people thinking critically and making creatively with AI. Exercises can be hands-on or analog, 15 minutes or a full day, beginner or advanced; the one hard requirement is that someone else can read your recipe and run it themselves. Examples of what might fit: hands-on experiments with specific AI tools (image generators, chatbots, code assistants, video models); analog activities that reveal computational logic through embodied practice; critical readings, watchings, or listenings with structured discussion; provocations, games, or role-plays that get at how AI sees, thinks, or makes; and exercises that help people figure out how they want to relate to AI in their own practice. WHAT YOU GET: your recipe formatted and credited to you in the first edition of Grain, a print copy when it lands, and an invite to the launch. Everything is published under CC BY-SA 4.0 so the work can travel and you keep authorship. FEE: this round offers a need-based fee for freelance and independent practitioners of EUR 150 per recipe; if making the time would be hard without it, email hello@aixdesign.co with your proposal. After this round the call stays open but the fee will not. LIVE SESSION: an optional 30-minute live session on Wednesday 12 August 2026 at 16:00 CEST lets you bring a rough exercise and turn it into a recipe on the spot with the Grain Recipe Converter (online, sign up at luma.com/84w148in). Submissions are open until the end of August 2026; Grain publishes March 2027. Submit via the form; questions to hello@aixdesign.co.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OpenAI Economic Research Exchange 2026
item.description:
Organisation: OpenAI

Location: Remote / global. Structured, project-based research collaborations with OpenAI Economic Research. No location restriction stated.

Award: USD 25,000 project grant plus a USD 7,500 monthly stipend per selected researcher.

Duration: Carefully scoped projects with defined milestones. OpenAI supports a portfolio across time horizons: short-term projects (2-6 months) using existing approved data, and medium-term studies (6-12 months) that track outcomes over time or test specific interventions.

Deadline: Closed 05 Jul 2026

The OpenAI Economic Research Exchange is a new program supporting high-impact external research on the economic effects of AI. Through the Exchange, selected researchers conduct work via structured, project-based collaborations with OpenAI Economic Research, with the goal of producing credible, independent evidence on how AI is affecting workers, firms, institutions, and the broader economy. OpenAI is seeking projects that address important questions about the economic impacts of AI; proposals should explain how carefully governed, privacy-protected use of OpenAI tools and datasets can help answer these questions, extending beyond what traditional datasets alone allow, while maintaining clear safeguards for user privacy and responsible data use. Core questions include labor market effects (moving beyond AI exposure to measure which occupations are likely to see augmentation, displacement, new task creation, or employment growth as model capabilities improve) and measuring economic value (willingness to pay, revealed value, task-level time savings, avoided costs, quality-adjusted output, and durable productivity gains). Selected researchers undertake carefully scoped projects with defined milestones, data governance, and review processes. WHO SHOULD APPLY: researchers with strong empirical skills and relevant expertise in applied causal inference, measurement, labor economics, productivity, firms, education, entrepreneurship, public finance, regional economics, development, inequality, or related fields. EVALUATION: proposals are assessed on methodological rigor, feasibility, fit with Exchange priorities, clear milestones, and the potential to contribute credible external evidence on AI's economic impacts. The Exchange builds on OpenAI's broader measurement efforts, including OpenAI Signals. For more information see the Request for Proposals, or contact econresearch@openai.com. Applications close 5 July 2026; selected researchers will be notified by 31 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
